name: Gateways
description: Conditions (codewords, items, states) are consistent across passages and can be enforced by the Player-Narrator without leaks. Conditions are clear and in-world.

rubric.instructions: Evaluate gateway quality. Conditions must be consistent, clear, enforceable by the Player-Narrator, and expressed in-world without exposing mechanics.

criteria.name: condition_clarity
criteria.description: Each gateway states a single clear condition that can be understood in-world.

good: If you bear the foreman's token, proceed to section 42
If Kestrel trusts you, she reveals the truth

criteria.name: pn_enforceability
criteria.description: A plausible in-world way exists for the PN to enforce the condition without exposing internals.

good: Player shows the token when asked
PN can ask 'Has Kestrel vouched for you?'
bad: No way to check condition in-world

criteria.name: condition_consistency
criteria.description: Negative conditions don't contradict positive checks elsewhere. Conditions are consistent across the topology.

bad: Section A requires token, Section B says 'unless you have the token'

failure_guidance: Rewrite ambiguous conditions in diegetic, in-world terms. Add catch-up or fail-forward beats for hidden unwinnable states. Ensure the Player-Narrator can enforce gates using world-consistent dialogue.
